plain_language.what_this_is: The ONE detector notice the release gate tolerates: the two known oversized source files, owner-accepted, split deferred. Acceptance is by EXACT full-string set equality — the bloat detector must report signal 'notice' with EXACTLY these two item strings and nothing else. Any change to wording, path, line count, threshold, or an added/removed item breaks the match and FAILS the gate.
plain_language.why_fail_closed: No substring or regex matching. This is deliberately brittle so a real change to those files (or a new large file) re-fails the gate and forces a fresh, deliberate acceptance instead of silently staying green.
plain_language.how_to_update: If those files legitimately change, re-run scripts/detect-bloat.ps1 and paste the new exact item strings here — an explicit, reviewable owner decision.
